Original CIE-based blackbody radiation color data underlying the Helland approximation
Fractals, chaos, complex systems — conceptual foundation for generative visuals

contributions.description: Foundational Indian astronomical treatise (~400 CE). Source for 27 Nakshatras, junction stars (yogataras), Vimsottari Dasha ruler cycle, and sidereal framework.

4 Yugas: cycle lengths, ratios, Maha-Yuga/Kalpa/Manvantara structure (Ch. 12)
Yuga position calculation, Kali Yuga epoch 3102 BCE
Panchanga five-limb system: Tithi, Vara, Yoga, Karana computation

contributions.description: Open-source prayer time algorithms and reference implementation. Method parameter tables (MWL, ISNA, Egypt, Makkah, Karachi), Asr shadow ratio formulas, hour angle approach for Fajr/Isha twilight.

references.note: Prayer time algorithms: hour angle, Asr shadow, 5 calculation methods

contributions.description: 5th-century Theravada commentary systematizing Abhidhamma cosmology. Source for maha-kalpa structure (4 phases), antarakalpa lifespan cycles, and cosmic time hierarchy.

references.note: Maha-kalpa phases, antarakalpa lifespan cycles, cosmic time hierarchy

contributions.description: Foundational Kabbalistic text (~1st-3rd century CE). Source for 3 mother letters (elements), 7 double letters (planets), 12 simple letters (zodiac), and 231 Gates (two-letter combinations).

contributors.name: Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn

contributions.description: Late 19th century synthesis mapping 22 Hebrew letters to Tarot Major Arcana, zodiac signs, planets, and elements on the Tree of Life paths.

references.note: Path-to-Sefirot connections, Tarot attributions, zodiac/planet/element correspondences

contributions.description: Comprehensive correspondence tables systematizing Golden Dawn attributions: Hebrew letters, paths, Tarot, zodiac, planets, elements, colors.

references.note: Book of Thoth: 22 Major Arcana names, color scales, attributions

contributions.description: Painted the 78 cards of the Thoth Tarot under Crowley's direction. Her geometric and symbolic art style directly informs the visual motifs and color choices in the tarot visual data module.

references.note: Thoth deck visual symbolism and geometric motifs

contributions.description: Published the Tree of Life path layout (Kircher Tree) that became the standard Western Kabbalistic topology: 22 paths connecting 10 Sefirot.

references.note: Kircher Tree topology: 22 path connections between Sefirot

contributions.work: The Harmonic Development of the Tide-Generating Potential
contributions.description: Systematic harmonic analysis of tides. Classified tidal constituents (M2, S2, N2, K1, O1, etc.) with periods and Doodson numbers. Foundation of modern tidal prediction.

references.note: 5 tidal constituents: M2, S2, N2, K1, O1 periods and harmonic analysis

contributions.description: Public domain tidal constituent amplitudes and periods. CO-OPS tide prediction methodology.

contributions.description: Revised TSI measurement from SORCE/TIM: 1361 W/m^2 at 1 AU

contributions.work: Applied Solar Energy: An Introduction (co-author with Marjorie Meinel)
contributions.description: Atmospheric transmittance model for surface irradiance: I_surface = I_toa * 0.7^(AM^0.678). Empirical fit for clear-sky solar radiation through Earth's atmosphere.

contributions.work: Applied Solar Energy: An Introduction (co-author with Aden Meinel)
contributions.description: Atmospheric transmittance model for surface irradiance
Standard atmospheric lapse rate of -6.5 C per 1000m (troposphere). Used worldwide for altitude-temperature relationship.

references.note: Lapse rate -6.5 C/km for elevation temperature correction

contributions.work: The I Ching and the Genetic Code
contributions.description: First systematic mapping of 64 DNA codons to 64 I Ching hexagrams via binary correspondence. Both systems enumerate 2^6=64 states: codon=3 bases x 2 bits, hexagram=6 lines x 1 bit.

references.note: Schonberger binary mapping: RNA bases to yin/yang lines (U=00, C=01, A=10, G=11)

contributions.description: Used shadow angles at Alexandria and Syene to compute Earth's circumference: 39,375 km (actual 40,075 km) — 1.7% error with a gnomon and geometry.
Compiled first systematic star catalog (~850 stars). Discovered precession of equinoxes (~46 arcsec/year). Measured tropical year to 6.5-minute accuracy.

references.note: Tropical year measurement and precession discovery

contributions.description: Earth circumference 39,968 km (0.27% error). Sidereal year 365.25858 days (3.2-min error). Equation of center 1200 years before Kepler. Sine tables. Earth rotates on axis.

references.note: Earth circumference, sidereal year, lunar month measurements

contributions.description: Lunar month 29.530582 days (0.6-second error). Rules for zero and negative numbers. Interpolation formula.
Established the fixed Hebrew calendar (359 CE). Molad (lunar month) 29.530594 days — 0.4-second monthly error. Still used today.
Astronomical tables used for 400+ years. Sine tables to 4 decimal places. Algorithms so foundational the word 'algorithm' derives from his name.

contributions.work: On the Sizes and Distances of the Sun and Moon
contributions.description: First geometric method for measuring Earth-Sun distance. Estimated 19x lunar distance (actual ~389x). Method was correct; measurement limited by naked-eye precision.
Sidereal year 365.25636 days — essentially exact match to modern value. Star catalog of 1018 stars. Built 40-meter sextant at Samarkand.

references.note: Sidereal year measurement — essentially exact (1437 CE)

contributions.description: Tropical year 365.2436 days (2-min error). Built world's first astronomical clock tower with escapement mechanism.

references.note: Tropical year measurement via clock tower (1088 CE)

contributions.description: Heliocentric model. Sidereal year 365.25671 days. Reframed the entire solar system.
Star positions accurate to 1 arcminute — most accurate naked-eye observations ever recorded. Enabled Kepler's laws.
Advised Julius Caesar on calendar reform (46 BCE). Designed 365.25-day solar year with leap year every 4 years.
Proposed the World Calendar (1930): 4 identical quarters of 91 days + Worldsday. Nearly adopted by League of Nations 1937.
Proposed 13 months of 28 days + Year Day (1902). 13th month 'Sol' between June and July. Used by Kodak internally 1928-1989.
Four Noble Truths, Eightfold Path. Buddhist calendar and Uposatha observance days derive from his teachings.

references.note: Buddhist system origin node in knowledge graph

contributions.description: Primary text of Kabbalah. Systematized the Sefirot, paths, and Four Worlds framework.

references.note: Kabbalah origin node — influenced Isaac Luria

contributions.description: Developed the concepts of tzimtzum (contraction), shevirat ha-kelim (breaking of vessels), and tikkun (repair). Transformed Kabbalistic thought.

references.note: Kabbalah transmission node — influenced by Moses de Leon

contributions.description: Promulgated the Gregorian calendar (1582). Deleted 10 days, adjusted leap year rules. Calendar still used worldwide.

references.note: Gregorian system node in knowledge graph

contributions.description: Originated the method of transposing planetary orbital periods to audible frequencies via octave doubling (f × 2^n). Derived Earth year = 136.1 Hz (C#, 'Om' frequency), Earth day = 194.18 Hz (G), synodic month = 210.42 Hz (G#).

references.note: Planetary frequency mapping via octave transposition (Cosmic Octave method)

contributions.work: Secular terms of classical planetary theories
contributions.description: High-precision 10th-degree polynomial for mean obliquity of the ecliptic, valid over 10000 years from J2000.0. Published in Astronomy and Astrophysics Vol. 157, p. 68.
Computus algorithm for Easter date calculation (Gregorian calendar), devised in 1876, published pp. 73-74. Valid from 1583 onward with no exceptions, unlike Gauss's method.
Comprehensive study of Egyptian decan star groups, diagonal star clocks, and astronomical ceiling texts. Established the standard transliteration system for the 36 decan names.
Co-author with Neugebauer. Identified decan star groups in coffin lids, temple ceilings, and papyri. Established chronological framework for Egyptian astronomical observation.

contributions.work: It's About Time: Ancient Egyptian Cosmology
contributions.description: Modern star identifications for Egyptian decans, correlating ancient star group descriptions with contemporary astronomical catalogs.

contributions.work: In Search of Cosmic Order: Selected Essays on Egyptian Archaeoastronomy
contributions.description: Decan-star correlation survey establishing consensus identifications for Egyptian decan groups with modern stars and constellations.
Definitive scholarship on the Zoroastrian Yazdgerdi calendar: epoch, month/day names, Gatha days, seasonal festivals, and calendar reform history.

references.note: Yazdgerdi calendar structure, epoch, month/day naming conventions

contributions.description: Data visualization principles: escaping flatland (multidimensional data on 2D), micro/macro readings, layering and separation, small multiples, 1+1=3 interference effect, Imhof's 4 color rules, 20-30 color limit, data-ink ratio, nature's palette strategy.

references.note: Complete extraction: 6 chapters, layering/separation/color/small multiples principles

contributions.description: 4 color rules for information display: bright colors sparingly on muted backgrounds, avoid adjacent light bright colors, large areas muted / small areas bright, interweave colors carpet-fashion for unity.

references.note: Imhof's 4 rules quoted via Tufte pp. 82-83

contributions.description: Systematic color interaction study: simultaneous contrast mechanism, subtraction rule, Weber-Fechner perceptual law application to color, color relativity (8 illusion types), Schopenhauer area ratios, three color polarities (light-dark, warm-cool, wet-dry), rejection of mechanical harmony rules in favor of quantity-based color relationships.

references.note: Full 26-chapter extraction: Q1-Q5 on contrast, relativity, temperature, quantitative rules, harmony

contributions.work: The Laws of Contrast of Colour
contributions.description: Pioneering simultaneous contrast research. Graduated grey scale method. Foundation for Impressionist color theory and all subsequent color interaction study.

references.note: Referenced by Albers Ch XX for graduated scale method

contributions.description: Color quantity ratios for visual balance from Goethe's 6-part color circle: Y:O:R:V:B:G = 3:4:6:9:8:6. Complementary balance areas: lightest color gets smallest area, darkest gets largest.

references.note: Schopenhauer area ratios quoted by Albers Ch XVI

contributions.description: Discovery that changing or adding a single color can transform an entire color composition. A special case of optical mixture, distinct from simultaneous contrast.

references.note: Bezold Effect documented by Albers Ch XIII

contributors.name: Ernst Heinrich Weber & Gustav Theodor Fechner

contributions.description: Perception follows logarithmic function of stimulus: geometric physical progression produces arithmetically equal perceptual steps. Applies to light, color, sound, weight, temperature. Foundation for perceptually uniform scales.

references.note: Weber-Fechner Law applied to color by Albers Ch XX

contributions.work: A Short History of Nearly Everything
contributions.description: Deep time scale comparisons, geochronology timeline, Big Bang nucleosynthesis narrative, extinction data, cosmic distance analogies. Source of vivid metaphors for earth_drama and fun_facts modules.

references.note: Scale comparisons, geochronology, fun facts extraction

contributions.work: Age of meteorites and the Earth
contributions.description: Definitive measurement of Earth's age at 4.55 billion years using lead isotope ratios from Canyon Diablo meteorite. Also discovered atmospheric lead contamination from leaded gasoline.
Calculated background extinction rate of 1 species per 4 years on average throughout biological history. Framework for measuring human impact on biodiversity (120,000x background rate).

references.note: Extinction rate data for fun_facts module
